codeReview.summary: The reviewed commit satisfies the feature contract: it renders a white-at-bottom 8x8 board from chess.js FEN state, shows all starting pieces with distinct glyphs, places file/rank labels correctly, preserves square aspect ratio through CSS, and updates the home page to a responsive board-centered layout with reserved side-panel space. I did not find any blocking correctness or regression issues in the feature diff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker skill still tells workers to run frontend commands from a nonexistent `web/` subdirectory, while this repo and services manifest run Next.js from the repository root. Future workers will have to rediscover the correct commands unless the skill is updated.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/frontend-worker/SKILL.md:96-120 shows `cd web && pnpm dev/build/lint`; .factory/services.yaml:4-5 and 30-33 define `pnpm dev --turbopack --port 3001` and other commands from the repo root.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker skill documents an outdated `useChessEngine` API shape, which no longer matches the actual hook exported by the codebase. That makes the skill unreliable as a reference for frontend engine integration.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/frontend-worker/SKILL.md:69-72 describes `{ isReady, isSearching, bestMove, evaluation, search, stop }`; commit 9388f8a `src/hooks/useChessEngine.ts`:11-17 and 153-159 actually return `{ isReady, isThinking, error, searchBestMove, evaluatePosition }`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker verification procedure does not explain what to do when the required dev server port is already occupied, even though the mission constrains website work to port 3001. The worker had to reuse an already-running approved server after hitting `EADDRINUSE`, and that fallback should be captured in the skill.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoffs/2026-03-31T20-34-04-521Z__website-board-component__a9c3e7f5-ecdd-4f2f-8e9b-90e05a1842b4.json -> handoff.skillFeedback.deviations[0] and suggestedChanges[0]; .factory/skills/frontend-worker/SKILL.md:94-114 only describes starting a fresh dev server.

summary: Pass. The board-component feature meets its rendering and responsive-layout requirements, and I found no blocking code issues. Shared-state follow-up is needed only for stale frontend-worker skill guidance.
